name: bioethics_communications_outage_forces_harbor_tru_loy
premise: A Bioethics ethics consult line is in a fast-moving incident involving bioethics communications outage forces harbor tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before markets open. Every delay increases consent, harm minimization, and precedent. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. This decision will be treated as precedent unless you explicitly time-box it. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Mira Ibrahim
agents.identity: I am Mira Ibrahim, accountable for a defensible call in Bioethics when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Zara Chandra
agents.identity: I am Zara Chandra, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Bioethics.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before markets open. Evidence is partial. Mira Ibrahim must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Mira Ibrahim do?
